subject: Re: Problem with reconstruct sub-tomograms
body:
If you are running Warp <= v1.09, then run relion_convert_star on the 
particle.star written out by Warp to convert it from RELIONv3 to RELIONv3.1 
format.

Hope that helps,
-Tom


On Monday, November 21, 2022 at 11:25:34 PM UTC-8 abett...@126.com wrote:

> Hi all,
> I reconstructed sub-tomograms in Warp, and the generated particles.star 
> file doesn't contain the necessary variables for Relion3.1: rlnPixelSize, 
> rlnVoltage and rlnSpherical Aberration. Therefore, when I input the star 
> file to run Class3D in Relion3.1, it doesn't work.
> How can I fix it?
> Thanks in advance!
>
> The star file only contains the following columns:
>
> rlnCoordinatex #1
>
> rlnCoordinatey #2
>
> rlnCoordinateZ #3
>
> rlnAngleRot #4
>
> rlnAngleTilt #5
>
> rLnAnglePst #6
>
> rlnMicrographName #7
>
> rlnAutopickFigure0fMerit #8
>
> rInMagnification #9
>
> rlnDetectorPixelSize #10
>
> rlnCtfMaxResolution #11
>
> rnImageName #12
>
> _rUnCtfImage #13
>
>
